name: Herbed Roast Leg of Lamb
recipeIngredient: 1/4 pound red pearl onions or white pearl onions
6 pounds leg of lamb, trimmed of excess fat
recipeInstructions:
1. Pearl onions: Blanch in boiling water for 3 minutes, then drain and peel.
2. To make the lamb:
a. In a large roasting pan, stir together tomatoes, mushrooms, bell pepper, yellow onion, shallots, garlic, pearl onions, rosemary, oregano, thyme, 1/4 cup olive oil, and salt and pepper to taste.
b. Arrange the leg of lamb (patted dry) on top of the vegetable mixture, drizzle with remaining oil, and season with salt and pepper.
c. Roast in a preheated 450°F oven for 15 minutes, then reduce temperature to 350°F and roast for 1 hour and 20 more minutes, or until lamb reaches 145°F on a meat thermometer (medium-rare).
3. Transfer lamb to a cutting board, cover loosely with foil, and let stand for 15 minutes.
4. Reserve pearl onions for mixed baby vegetables.
5. To make the mixed vegetables:
a. In boiling salted water, cook zucchini, pattypan squash, carrots, and haricots verts until crisp-tender; cook potato (peeled and cut into 1/2-inch dice) until cooked through, transferring vegetables as they are cooked with a slotted spoon to an ice-water bath to stop cooking.
b. Drain vegetables and pat dry. Vegetables can be prepared up to this point 1 day in advance and kept covered and chilled.
c. In a large skillet, heat oil over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking; saute zucchini, pattypan squash, carrots, haricots verts, onions for 3 minutes or until heated through.
d. Stir in potato, herbs, salt, and pepper to taste; saute mixture for 1 minute.
User safety: Ensure the skillet is cool before washing.
